name: Student First Trnsprtn Inc
recent_news: No public M&A or news; very small entity with minimal web footprint; not to be confused with First Student Inc. (EQT-owned).
fit_notes: Very small (~12 employees, ~$535K rev) Twin Cities operator; potentially a micro-tuck-in only if it holds Saint Paul/special-needs routes — too small to move the needle individually but could be folded into a Minnesota platform; ownership and fleet need primary-source confirmation.

name: Coastal Transportation
recent_news: No public ownership or M&A news; very small entity with no web presence; not affiliated with cargo/petroleum 'Coastal Transportation' brands.
fit_notes: Micro-operator (~10 employees, ~$515K rev) in East Brunswick — too small as a standalone tuck-in for Summit; NJ market is heavily fragmented family-owned but this name lacks any verifiable district contracts; only relevant if it holds dedicated SPED routes.

name: Amethyst School Bus Svc
recent_news: No public news, website, or M&A activity; LAUSD has transitioned much of its yellow-bus business to Zum.
fit_notes: Micro-operator (~10 employees, ~$502K rev) in San Fernando; LAUSD's 2022 mega-contract shift to Zum makes small CA contractors structurally squeezed — unattractive as a tuck-in unless it holds private-school or SPED contracts independent of LAUSD.

name: B & B Busing-Just For Kids
districts_or_customers: Junction City & Manhattan KS communities; door-to-door student transportation, field trips, corporate shuttles
recent_news: BBB lists business as 'Out-of-Business known or suspected' (alert opened 2010), yet FMCSA shows USDOT 955854 ACTIVE with 22 power units and 18 drivers as of 2026 and the company website (bnbbusing.com) is live; conflicting signals suggest a long-tenured single-owner operator still running.
fit_notes: ~37-year-old single-owner (Bryan Bennett) Kansas operator with ~22 buses serving Junction City/Manhattan (Fort Riley area) — classic high-succession family tuck-in profile; BBB 'out of business' flag plus active FMCSA/website warrants a direct outreach call; KS is outside Summit's densest footprint but Fort Riley military-adjacent routes could be attractive.

name: Gallant Limousine
districts_or_customers: Greater Cleveland / Portage County OH area (unconfirmed)
recent_news: No public news, website, or M&A activity located; name suggests livery/limousine rather than dedicated yellow-bus school operator; not affiliated with 'Gallant Knight Limousine'.
fit_notes: Likely a small livery/limo operator that may run some SPED/alternative student-transport contracts (van-based) rather than a true yellow-bus contractor — not a core Summit tuck-in target; could be relevant only if it holds Ohio district SPED contracts on dedicated vehicles, otherwise screen out.
